fresh_facts.claim: US conducted strikes on Iranian nuclear facilities on April 7, 2026, using B-2 bombers and a 30,000-pound bunker buster bomb against Fordow, Natanz, and Isfahan sites.
President Trump claimed Iranian nuclear facilities were 'totally obliterated' in the April 7 strikes.
Iran announced the 98th wave of 'Operation True Promise 4' on April 8, 2026, including IRGC Navy cruise missile strikes on multiple US and Israeli targets.
Iranian IRGC Navy conducted cruise missile strikes on the container ship SDN7, causing extensive fire.
Iranian forces intercepted multiple drones including Orbit, Hermes 900, and MQ9 aircraft over Iranian territory.
Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu praised the US strikes on April 7 as a 'very successful attack' embodying 'peace through strength.'
Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei was killed in US-Israeli strikes on February 28, 2026.
Mojtaba Khamenei was appointed successor to Supreme Leader by the Assembly of Experts following Ali Khamenei's death.
Reports indicate a leadership vacuum in Iran with Mojtaba Khamenei possibly injured and no unified command.
A two-week US-Iran ceasefire took effect on April 8, 2026, with Israel also agreeing to the terms.
US President Trump postponed attacks on Iranian energy infrastructure and power plants for at least five days.
US envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner engaged a top Iranian official by phone regarding ceasefire negotiations.
Iran received a 15-point US peace plan via Pakistan that includes reopening the Strait of Hormuz, missile limits, sanctions relief, and nuclear rollback.
President Masoud Pezeshkian publicly accused IRGC commanders of sabotaging the ceasefire, exposing top-level rifts.
Iranian hardliners labeled ex-Foreign Minister Zarif a 'traitor' for urging war's end and called for his arrest.
Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr, a hardline ex-IRGC deputy, replaced the killed Ali Larijani as Security Council chief.
War has severed Iran's Dubai trade lifeline and triggered 'violent' economic shocks from declining checks and balances.
A drone attack damaged Saudi Arabia's East-West oil pipeline hours after the ceasefire took effect.
Trump plans to remove Iran's nuclear material and considers extracting enriched uranium.
Intensive pre-deadline strikes killed 49 civilians and injured 58 on Monday per HRANA reporting.
Security forces executed at least five protesters last week on protest- and security-related charges.
Iran affirmed continued defense using 'all means necessary' against US and Israeli actions.
Parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf denied that US-Iran direct talks occurred.
Combined force strikes disrupted Iranian railways and roads, potentially limiting weapons transport, per April 7 analysis.
Iran insists the war ends only on its terms, including cessation of all-front fighting and non-aggression guarantees.
Mediators including Egypt, Pakistan, and Turkey have conveyed messages between US and Iran.

regime_fall.current_status: Day 42 crisis has reached inflection point with US nuclear strikes on Iranian facilities (April 7) followed by ceasefire announcement (April 8) and simultaneous Iranian military response, while Iran's leadership exhibits severe fracture between hardliners rejecting ceasefire and reformists seeking de-escalation, compounded by uncertainty over Mojtaba Khamenei's succession viability and potential leadership vacuum.
regime_fall.last_major_event: US and Iran agreed to two-week ceasefire on April 8, 2026, with Trump postponing further infrastructure strikes for five days, while Iran simultaneously launched cruise missile strikes and rejected formal ceasefire terms, creating contradictory signals indicating internal regime conflict over war continuation versus negotiation.
